tasks.name: AI Content Calendar with Auto-Publishing Pipeline
tasks.text_content:
## Frontend
- Enhance existing `/editorial-calendar` page with full calendar functionality
- Monthly/weekly/daily calendar view (use FullCalendar or custom React calendar)
- Drag-and-drop blog posts onto calendar dates to schedule
- Color-coded cards by status: Draft (gray), Scheduled (blue), Published (green), Failed (red)
- Click calendar slot to create new post with pre-filled publish date
- AI content planner: "Generate 30-day content plan" button
  - Analyzes tracked keywords, competitor gaps, seasonal trends
  - Suggests topics with optimal publish dates
  - One-click "Generate All" to create draft posts for entire month
- Post preview on hover (title, excerpt, featured image, SEO score)
- Bulk actions: select multiple posts -> reschedule, publish, delete
- Publishing queue sidebar showing upcoming scheduled posts
- Auto-publish status indicator with retry on failure
- Weekly email digest of scheduled vs published content (via backend)

## Backend
- Enhance existing `BlogController` with calendar-specific endpoints:
- `GET /api/blog/website/{websiteId}/calendar` - Posts grouped by date for calendar view
- `POST /api/blog/website/{websiteId}/content-plan` - AI generate 30-day content plan
- `POST /api/blog/website/{websiteId}/bulk-schedule` - Schedule multiple posts at once
- `PUT /api/blog/{postId}/reschedule` - Change scheduled date
- `ContentPlannerService` using GPT-4o:
  - Analyze existing content for gaps
  - Use tracked keywords for topic generation
  - Consider seasonal relevance and search trends
  - Output: array of {topic, keywords, suggestedDate, estimatedImpact}
- Enhance Hangfire scheduled publishing:
  - Retry logic on WordPress publish failure (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
  - Failure notification stored in `PublishLog` entity
  - Weekly digest email via `EmailService`

## Impact
- Content consistency is #1 factor in SEO success - calendar enforces it
- AI content planner eliminates "what should I write about" problem
- Auto-publishing pipeline removes manual work from content workflow
- 30-day plan generation is a premium feature competitors charge $50+/mo for

## Testing
1. Verify calendar renders with existing scheduled/published posts
2. Drag-drop post to new date, verify schedule updates in backend
3. Generate 30-day content plan, verify topic relevance and date spread
4. Test "Generate All" creates draft posts with correct scheduled dates
5. Verify auto-publish fires on scheduled time (test with 1-minute future date)
6. Test publish failure retry (simulate WordPress connection timeout)
7. Verify bulk reschedule updates all selected posts
8. Test weekly digest email content and formatting

tasks.name: Complete AI Feature Retry Logic (SEO/FAQ/Blog/Image)
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
## Value Proposition
AI features (SEO analysis, FAQ generation, blog posts, image generation) fail silently or show cryptic errors

TECHNICAL DETAILS
## Value Proposition
AI features (SEO analysis, FAQ generation, blog posts, image generation) fail silently or show cryptic errors. Need retry logic, better error handling, and user-friendly error messages.

## Current State
TODO task - AI features unreliable

## Broken Aspects
No retry logic, poor error handling, OpenAI API failures not caught

## Acceptance Criteria
- All AI calls wrapped in try-catch with retry logic
- 3 retry attempts with exponential backoff
- User-friendly error messages (not raw API errors)
- Fallback behavior when AI unavailable
- Logging for debugging AI failures
- Rate limit handling (429 errors)

## Estimated Effort
High (8 hours)

## Files to Modify
- backend/SEOGod.Services/AI/SEOAnalysisService.cs
- backend/SEOGod.Services/AI/FAQGenerationService.cs
- backend/SEOGod.Services/AI/BlogPostService.cs
- backend/SEOGod.Services/AI/ImageGenerationService.cs




---
**Category:** AI Reliability
**ROI Score:** 91/100
**Generated:** 2026-03-11

---
Refined by ClickUp Refinement Agent on 2026-03-11 20:01

tasks.name: Add WordPress Plugin Detection Feedback During Onboarding
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
## Value Proposition
Onboarding wizard doesn't immediately show plugin detected status

TECHNICAL DETAILS
## Value Proposition
Onboarding wizard doesn't immediately show plugin detected status. Users wait without visual feedback. Should display detection result instantly after entering WordPress URL.

## Current State
DONE task but may need enhancement

## Broken Aspects
Real-time plugin detection feedback

## Acceptance Criteria
- Plugin detection runs immediately on URL entry
- Visual indicator shows 'plugin detected' or 'plugin not found'
- Detected status appears within 2 seconds
- Clear next steps shown based on detection result
- Error handling for unreachable sites

## Estimated Effort
Medium (3 hours)

## Files to Modify
- frontend/src/components/Onboarding/OnboardingWizard.tsx
- backend/SEOGod.API/Controllers/WordPressWebsiteController.cs




---
**Category:** Onboarding UX
**ROI Score:** 88/100
**Generated:** 2026-03-11

---

tasks.name: Fix FAQ Toggle Form Disappearing
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
## Value Proposition
When turning on FAQs in FAQ component, entire form disappears

TECHNICAL DETAILS
## Value Proposition
When turning on FAQs in FAQ component, entire form disappears. Likely state management issue in React component or CSS display:none bug. Renders FAQ feature unusable.

## Current State
BLOCKED task - FAQ toggle makes form vanish

## Broken Aspects
React state management, conditional rendering logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- Toggling FAQs on/off keeps form visible
- Toggle state persists correctly
- Form remains functional after toggle
- No console errors during toggle
- Smooth transition animation (optional)

## Estimated Effort
Low (2 hours)

## Files to Modify
- frontend/src/pages/FAQPage.tsx
- frontend/src/components/FAQManager.tsx

## Testing Steps
- Open FAQ page
- Toggle FAQs on
- Verify form still visible
- Toggle off and on multiple times
- Check React DevTools for state

---
**Category:** Critical Bug Fix
**ROI Score:** 95/100
**Generated:** 2026-03-11

---
Refined by ClickUp Refinement Agent on 2026-03-11 20:01

tasks.name: Fix FAQ Generation 500 Error
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
## Value Proposition
FAQ generation component throws 500 error

TECHNICAL DETAILS
## Value Proposition
FAQ generation component throws 500 error. Backend API endpoint likely has unhandled exception in AI service call or database transaction. Users cannot generate FAQs, breaking core feature.

## Current State
BLOCKED task - FAQ generation fails with 500 error

## Broken Aspects
AI FAQ generation API endpoint crashes, no error handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- FAQ generation completes without 500 error
- Proper error handling with user-friendly messages
- AI service failures return graceful error
- Database transactions properly wrapped in try-catch
- Logging added for debugging future issues

## Estimated Effort
Medium (4 hours)

## Files to Modify
- backend/SEOGod.API/Controllers/FAQController.cs
- backend/SEOGod.Services/AI/FAQGenerationService.cs

## Testing Steps
- Generate FAQs for test URL
- Verify AI response is handled correctly
- Test with invalid URL
- Check error logs for proper logging
- Confirm UI shows meaningful error message

---

**Generated:** 2026-03-11

tasks.name: when im logging in to the site from wordpress i still see this message in the app dashboard: WordPress plugin is active but not connected to this site.  Connect Plugin
WordPress plugin: auto-deploy naar WP marketplace bij nieuwe release-tag
tasks.text_content:
**Doel:** Bij elke push naar master + een versie-tag (bv. v1.2.3) wordt de WordPress plugin automatisch gepackaged en geupload naar de WordPress.org plugin directory via SVN.

**Hoe WordPress.org deployment werkt:**
- WordPress.org gebruikt SVN (niet Git) voor plugin-distributie
- Nieuw versie = commit naar SVN trunk + tag in SVN

**GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/wp-release.yml):**
- Trigger: push met tag v*.*.* op master
- Steps:
  1. Checkout code
  2. Stel versie in plugin headers in op basis van git tag
  3. SVN checkout van de plugin directory op WordPress.org
  4. Kopieer plugin-bestanden naar SVN trunk
  5. Maak SVN tag aan voor de nieuwe versie
  6. SVN commit met WP.org credentials (opgeslagen als GitHub Secrets)

**Secrets nodig in GitHub:**
- WP_SVN_USERNAME
- WP_SVN_PASSWORD

**Acceptatiecriteria:**
- Tag pushen triggert workflow
- Plugin verschijnt met juiste versie op WordPress.org
- Geen handmatige SVN-stappen nodig
tasks.description:

- WordPress.org gebruikt SVN (niet Git) voor plugin-distributie
- Nieuw versie = commit naar SVN trunk + tag in SVN

**GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/wp-release.yml):**
- Trigger: push met tag v*.*.* op master
- Steps:
  1. Checkout code
  2. Stel versie in plugin headers in op basis van git tag
  3. SVN checkout van de plugin directory op WordPress.org
  4. Kopieer plugin-bestanden naar SVN trunk
  5. Maak SVN tag aan voor de nieuwe versie
  6. SVN commit met WP.org credentials (opgeslagen als GitHub Secrets)

**Secrets nodig in GitHub:**
- WP_SVN_USERNAME
- WP_SVN_PASSWORD

**Acceptatiecriteria:**
- Tag pushen triggert workflow
- Plugin verschijnt met juiste versie op WordPress.org
- Geen handmatige SVN-stappen nodig

tasks.name: in the website page where the urls are being displayed each row has an item for FAQ that says ON! or OFF! it seems that each item is on by default that is wrong it should be on only if the faqs for that item are switched on. and i want to be able to click on it so that it turns from off to on or from on to off
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
Fix FAQ toggle display logic - currently shows "ON" by default when it should reflect actual FAQ status.

FRONTEND
- File: frontend/src/pages/URLsPage.tsx or DashboardPage.tsx (URL list component)
- Logic: FAQ toggle should check if FAQs actually exist for the URL
- Display: Show "ON" only if FAQ content exists, "OFF" if not
- Interaction: Clicking should toggle FAQ generation status

BACKEND
- Endpoint: GET /api/urls/{id}/faq-status or similar
- Return whether FAQ content exists for this URL
- Endpoint: POST /api/urls/{id}/faq-toggle to enable/disable

IMPACT
- User Benefit: Accurate FAQ status display
- Priority: Medium - UI accuracy issue

TESTING
- Load URLs page, verify FAQ status reflects reality
- Click toggle, verify it changes state correctly
- Generate FAQs, verify status updates to ON

tasks.name: When I click on seo in the website urls page I want to see a popup window similarly to when i press faqs but this time it should show the seo advice for that url. with a generate button if no advice has been given yet.
tasks.text_content:
SUMMARY
Clicking SEO button in URL list should show popup with SEO analysis, similar to FAQ popup pattern.

FRONTEND
- Component: Create SEOAnalysisPopup.tsx modal component
- Trigger: onClick SEO button for URL
- Content:
  - SEO score and summary
  - Button: "Generate SEO Analysis" (if not exists)
  - Display: SEO recommendations if exists
  - Actions: Refresh analysis, apply suggestions
- File: frontend/src/components/SEOAnalysisPopup.tsx
- Integration: frontend/src/pages/URLsPage.tsx

BACKEND
- Endpoint: GET /api/seo/analysis/{urlId}
- Returns: SEO analysis data or null if not generated
- Endpoint: POST /api/seo/generate/{urlId} to trigger generation

IMPACT
- User Benefit: Quick SEO analysis access without navigation
- Priority: Medium - UX improvement
- Related: Task 869cd028t (URL popup)

TESTING
- Click SEO button for URL
- Verify popup appears
- If no analysis, verify "Generate" button works
- If analysis exists, verify it displays correctly
